title: Crowding out
description: Practice what you have learned about the crowding out effect and the impact of budget deficits on the real interest rate in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the most likely way a government will finance deficit spending?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Borrowing
Decrease the discount rate
Print money
Increase public saving
Buy bonds

assessment_items.question_content:
A government that has a budget balance enters a recession and decides to engage in expansionary fiscal policy. 

**Which of the following best describes what will most likely happen as a result of the expansionary fiscal policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The government runs a budget deficit
The government runs a budget surplus
The government buys bonds
The central bank buys bonds
Government borrowing decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Burginville has run a deficit of \$20 million which it intends to pay for by issuing new government bonds. 

**What impact will this action have on the bond market and the market for loanable funds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Bond prices and interest rates are inversely related.

choices.content: The supply of bonds in the bond market increases; the demand for funds in the loanable funds market increases
The supply of bonds in the bond market is not impacted; the demand for funds in the loanable funds market increases
The supply of bonds in the bond market increases; the demand for funds in the loanable funds market is not impacted
The supply of bonds in the bond market decreases; the demand for funds in the loanable funds market decreases
The supply of bonds in the bond market decreases; the demand for funds in the loanable funds market is not affected

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which model would be used to show the impact of government deficit spending on the real interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market for loanable funds
The money market
The AD-AS model
The production possibilities curve
The circular flow diagram

assessment_items.question_content:
An economy has a budget deficit, and you want to show the impact of the budget deficit on the real interest rate. 

**What model would you use, and what would be the impact on the interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The loanable funds market; real interest rate increases
The loanable funds market; real interest rate decreases
The money market; the real interest rate decreases
The money market; the real interest rate is not impacted
The AD-AS model; the real interest rate is not impacted

assessment_items.question_content:
**Under which circumstance is crowding out most likely to be a concern?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When an economy is operating near full employment
When an economy is experiencing a severe recession
When an economy can produce additional savings easily
When an economy has a current rate of unemployment that is higher than the natural rate
When there is deflation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the term “crowding out” refer to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When government borrowing increases, private sector investment spending decreases
When private sector borrowing increases, government borrowing decreases
When one private industry within an economy borrows more, other sectors of the economy borrow less
When there is an increase in unemployment, spending on automatic stabilizers increases
When there is a decrease in tax revenue, revenue from automatic stabilizers decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the most likely long-run impact of government deficits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A smaller stock of capital
Higher potential output
Less government borrowing
A higher money multiplier
More private investment

assessment_items.question_content:
Read “→” as “leads to” in the options below.

**Which of the following best describes the chain of events in the relationship between deficits and potential output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Deficit → higher real interest rates → less private investment → smaller capital stock → slower growth of potential output
Deficit → higher real interest rates → more private investment → larger capital stock → faster growth of potential output
Deficits → lower real interest rates → more private investment → larger capital stock → faster growth of potential output
Deficits → lower real interest rates → less private investment → smaller capital stock → slower growth of potential output
Deficits → higher real interest rates → less private investment → smaller capital stock → faster growth of potential output

assessment_items.question_content:
The nation of Fantastica engaged in borrowing to finance deficit spending. 

**Which of the following would be the most likely impact on the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve in Fantastica?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: LRAS decreases (shifts to the left)
LRAS increases (shifts to the right)
LRAS will not change
LRAS will become vertical
LRAS will become horizontal

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Burginville has been running budget deficits which it paid for by borrowing.

**What is the likely impact of government borrowing on spending on capital goods and economic growth in Burginville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A decrease in spending on capital goods; a decrease in economic growth
A decrease in spending on capital goods; no effect on economic growth
Increase in spending on capital goods; an increase in economic growth
Increase in spending on capital goods; no effect on economic growth
No effect on spending on capital goods or economic growth
